description_long: This is a tan-colored World War II-era German tank, likely a Panzer IV, featuring a large central turret with a long-barreled cannon and a mounted machine gun on top. The vehicle is equipped with heavy armor plating covering the entire body, including reinforced front and side panels, and has visible spare track links and tools attached to the hull. The tank runs on wide, rugged metal tracks with multiple road wheels, and the rear is fitted with cylindrical exhausts and storage bins. Distinctive military markings, such as crosses and numbers, are visible on the front and rear, along with a white tactical symbol. The surface is matte and shows signs of weathering, with a utilitarian design optimized for combat. The overall build is robust, with numerous hatches, periscopes, and external equipment mounts.
description: A tan, heavily armored German tank with a large turret, long cannon, and visible military markings, running on metal tracks with rugged detailing.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main cannon, frontal armor, German cross insignia, and a white tactical symbol.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the long barrel, side armor skirts, spare track links, tools, and the machine gun on the turret.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the cylindrical exhausts, storage bins, and the number '9527' painted on the back.
description_view_3: Right side view reveals additional side armor, tools, and the full length of the tracks and wheels.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One large cannon mounted on the central turret and one machine gun mounted on the turret roof.
attribute_armor_plating: Full body armor plating with reinforced and sloped panels on the front, sides, and turret; visible thickness and modular attachments.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: Solid tan paint scheme with weathering and dirt effects; no distinct camouflage pattern.
attribute_military_markings: Black German cross on the front left, white tactical symbol on the front right, and white number '9527' on the rear.
attribute_tire_type: Not applicable; vehicle uses metal tracks with visible tread and road wheels.
